title: [lichen_l2.c:865] memcpy uses sizeof(iface_link_addr) not LICHEN_L2_ADDR_LEN
description:
In lichen_l2_iface_init(), the memcpy copies using sizeof(iface_link_addr):

```c
memcpy(iface_link_addr, eui64, sizeof(iface_link_addr));
```

While this currently works (iface_link_addr is declared as uint8_t[LICHEN_L2_ADDR_LEN]), it creates a fragile coupling. If iface_link_addr's declaration ever changes to a pointer or different size, this will silently break.

The BUILD_ASSERT at line 42 ensures LICHEN_L2_ADDR_LEN == LICHEN_LORA_L2_ADDR_LEN, but doesn't guard against sizeof(iface_link_addr) drifting.

Recommendation: Use LICHEN_L2_ADDR_LEN explicitly to match the net_if_set_link_addr() call on line 868.

Impact: P3 code quality - currently works due to array declaration, but fragile.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed memcpy to use LICHEN_L2_ADDR_LEN constant instead of sizeof(iface_link_addr) for consistent address length handling
